Let me outline the code steps.

First, pre-process the character positions:

for (int i = 0; i < word1.size(); ++i) {
    char_positions[word1[i]].push_back(i);
}

Then, find the base subsequence:

vector<int> indices;
int ptr = 0; // pointer in word2
int prev = -1;

for (int i = 0; i < word2.size(); ++i) {
    char c = word2[i];
    if (!char_positions.count(c)) return {};
    // find the first index in char_positions[c] that is > prev

    if (it == positions.end()) return {};
    prev = *it;
    indices.push_back(prev);
}

Once we have indices, check the differences between the characters in word2 and the characters at indices in word1.

}

Else, proceed to try adjusting.

Now, for each position k in indices_diff, try to adjust.

Let me think of the code for this part.

For each k in indices_diff:

   original_prev = the index before k in the original indices. For example, if k is 0, original_prev is -1. If k is 1, original_prev is indices[0], etc.

   new_indices[k] = j;

           // no solution for this adjustment

       auto it = lower_bound(pos_list.begin(), pos_list.end(), new_prev + 1);

           // no solution for this adjustment
           break;
       }
       new_prev = *it;
       new_indices[new_k] = new_prev;
       new_k++;
       word2_pos++;
   }

   if (word2_pos == word2.size()) {
       // found a valid subsequence after adjustment.

       // Now check how many differences this new subsequence has.
